title: rosa canina f. ambigua
intro: Rosa canina f. ambigua, also known as the Scotch Rose or Dwarf Dog Rose, is a subspecies of Rosa canina, a perennial shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of vitamins A and C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina f. ambigua include its potential to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of jams, jellies, and other food products.
